 {"id":"git_safety_guard-78a","title":"[PACK] remote.rsync - rsync Pack","description":"# rsync Remote Sync Pack - PROMOTED TO TIER 1

## Why Tier 1 (Critical)
**This pack was promoted from Tier 3 to Tier 1** because `rsync --delete` is statistically one of the most common destructive command mistakes. Analysis of developer surveys, Stack Overflow questions, and incident reports shows this is a top-5 footgun.

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for rsync operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### CRITICAL: --delete variants
- `rsync --delete` - Deletes files at dest not in source
- `rsync --delete-before` - Delete before transfer
- `rsync --delete-during` - Delete during transfer  
- `rsync --delete-after` - Delete after transfer
- `rsync --delete-excluded` - Delete excluded files too
- `rsync --del` - Short alias for --delete

### Direction Mistakes
- Wrong source/dest direction can overwrite important files
- Empty source + --delete = wipes destination

## Safe Patterns
- `rsync -n` / `rsync --dry-run` - Preview only
- `rsync --list-only` - List without transfer
- `rsync -v` - Verbose (for inspection)
- `rsync` without --delete flags - Copy only, no deletion

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: Promoted to Tier 1 (was remote.*)
- Keywords: rsync
- CRITICAL: Must catch all --delete variants including short forms

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/packs/remote/rsync.rs`
- [ ] E2E tests in `scripts/e2e_tests/rsync.txt`
- [ ] Test all --delete variants (--delete, --del, --delete-before, etc.)
- [ ] Test --dry-run makes --delete safe
- [ ] Test edge cases (--delete in quotes, environment variables)
- [ ] Performance < 500µs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All --delete variants blocked
- [ ] --dry-run recognized as safe modifier
- [ ] Short forms (--del) caught
- [ ] Tests specifically cover delete variants
- [ ] Completion checklist passed","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:49.913668169-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:06:00.909791518-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:06:00.909791518-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.411726667-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.451532603-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.490080611-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.529928225-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.568774445-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.607980562-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.377343302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.422878134-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","title":"[PACK] monitoring.datadog - Datadog Pack","description":"# Datadog Monitoring Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Datadog CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### datadog-ci CLI
- `datadog-ci monitors delete`
- `datadog-ci dashboards delete`

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE api.datadoghq.com/.../monitor/`
- `curl -X DELETE api.datadoghq.com/.../dashboard/`
- `curl -X DELETE api.datadoghq.com/.../synthetics/`

### Terraform
- `terraform destroy` on datadog resources

## Safe Patterns
- `datadog-ci monitors get`
- `datadog-ci monitors list`
- API GET requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: monitoring.* (git_safety_guard-adw)
- Keywords: datadog-ci, datadoghq
- Note: Monitor deletion affects alerting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Monitor deletion blocked
- [ ] Dashboard deletion blocked
- [ ] API patterns detected
- [ ] Tests cover common Datadog operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:15.761125915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:52:15.06563067-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:52:15.06563067-05:00","close_reason":"Registered monitoring.datadog pack in PACK_ENTRIES array, fixed clippy lints, all lib tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.649889622-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.691085132-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.733814247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.775614535-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.818636562-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.860285024-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.088410289-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7cx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.134819517-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7e0","title":"E2E gap analysis + feature matrix","description":"## Objective

Inventory E2E coverage across hook + CLI + scan to identify missing scenarios.

## Scope

- Build a matrix of features vs E2E coverage.
- Include: hook allow/deny, pack enablement, allowlist layering, doctor, config precedence, explain, simulate, scan flows.
- Mark existing coverage from `scripts/e2e_test.sh` and `scripts/scan_precommit_e2e.sh`.

## Deliverables

- Markdown matrix with gaps + priority ordering.
- List of new scripts/tests required with rationale.

## Acceptance criteria

- Matrix is complete enough to drive implementation tasks.
- Gaps are classified by severity (P0/P1/P2).

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-7ey","title":"Feature: Dependabot Configuration for Automated Dependency Updates","description":"# Feature: Dependabot Configuration

## Background

DCG has no automated dependency update system. Dependencies are updated manually, which means:
- Security patches may be missed or delayed
- New features/performance improvements not adopted
- Technical debt accumulates silently

Dependabot is GitHubs built-in dependency update tool that:
- Automatically creates PRs for outdated dependencies
- Groups related updates together
- Provides security alerts for vulnerable dependencies

## Why This Matters for DCG

DCG uses several dependencies that receive regular updates:

| Dependency | Purpose | Update Frequency |
|------------|---------|------------------|
| serde/serde_json | JSON parsing | Monthly |
| fancy-regex | Pattern matching | Quarterly |
| colored | Terminal output | Quarterly |
| memchr | Fast string search | Quarterly |

Security-critical dependencies like serde_json need timely updates when vulnerabilities are found.

## Technical Approach

### Dependabot Configuration

Create `.github/dependabot.yml`:

```yaml
version: 2
updates:
  # Cargo dependencies
  - package-ecosystem: "cargo"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
    commit-message:
      prefix: "deps"
    groups:
      rust-dependencies:
        patterns:
          - "*"
        exclude-patterns:
          - "serde*"  # Update serde separately (breaking changes possible)
    
  # GitHub Actions
  - package-ecosystem: "github-actions"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 3
    commit-message:
      prefix: "ci"
```

### Why These Settings

**Weekly schedule**: Frequent enough to stay current, not so frequent as to be noisy

**Monday**: Updates at start of week, time to review before Friday

**PR limits**: Prevents overwhelming with updates (5 cargo, 3 actions)

**Grouping**: Groups minor updates together, keeps serde separate (more careful review needed)

**Commit prefixes**: deps: for cargo, ci: for actions - follows conventional commits

## Implementation Tasks

1. Create dependabot.yml configuration
2. Configure PR grouping strategy
3. Set up commit message prefixes
4. Document update review process

## Success Criteria

- [ ] dependabot.yml created and valid
- [ ] Cargo dependencies monitored weekly
- [ ] GitHub Actions monitored weekly
- [ ] PR grouping configured
- [ ] First automated PRs appear

## Considerations

### Breaking Changes

Some updates may have breaking changes. Dependabot PRs should:
1. Pass CI before merging
2. Be reviewed for changelog notes
3. Test manually if significant

### Security Updates

Dependabot creates security PRs immediately (not on schedule) for vulnerable dependencies. These should be prioritized.

### PR Review Burden

5 PRs/week max is manageable. If too noisy, can reduce to monthly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:24:04.173949685-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:24:04.173949685-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7ey","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-o6p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.519713914-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7f5","title":"Implement two-tier detection architecture (regex + ast-grep)","description":"# Two-Tier Architecture for Heredoc Detection

## Key Insight

The architecture uses a **two-tier approach** to balance performance with accuracy:

1. **Tier 1**: Use fast regex for heredoc detection (the `regex` crate uses Aho-Corasick for static compound patterns and SIMD - it's extremely optimized)
2. **Tier 2**: Once a heredoc is detected, use AST-aware parsing (ast-grep) rather than reimplementing structural analysis badly with regex

This ensures 99%+ of commands (which don't contain heredocs) pay only microseconds of overhead, while heredoc commands get proper structural analysis.

## Architecture

```
Command Input
     │
     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  TIER 1: Fast Regex Detection (μs)          │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  • regex crate (Aho-Corasick + SIMD)        │
│  • Static pattern set for heredoc markers   │
│  • Quick reject: 99%+ commands pass through │
│  • Cost: ~1-10 microseconds                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
     │
     │ heredoc detected?
     │
     ├──── NO ───▶ Continue to existing pattern matching
     │
     ▼ YES
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  TIER 2: AST-Aware Analysis (ms)            │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────  │
│  • ast-grep-core for structural parsing     │
│  • Language-specific pattern matching       │
│  • Only invoked when heredocs found         │
│  • Cost: ~1-5 milliseconds                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
     │
     ▼
  Block/Allow Decision
```

## Tier 1: Regex-Based Heredoc Detection

### Why `regex` crate?

The Rust `regex` crate is extraordinarily optimized:
- **Aho-Corasick**: Multi-pattern matching in O(n) time
- **SIMD**: Vectorized string operations (AVX2, SSE4.2)
- **Lazy DFA**: JIT-compiled automata
- **No backtracking**: Guaranteed linear time

### Heredoc Markers to Detect

```rust
use regex::RegexSet;

static HEREDOC_PATTERNS: LazyLock<RegexSet> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    RegexSet::new([
        // Bash heredocs
        r"<<-?\s*['\"]?\w+['\"]?",      // <<EOF, <<'EOF', <<"EOF", <<-EOF
        r"<<<",                           // Here-string

        // Inline script flags
        r"\b(python3?|ruby|perl|node)\s+-[ec]\s",
        r"\bsh\s+-c\s",
        r"\bbash\s+-c\s",

        // Pipe to interpreter
        r"\|\s*(python3?|ruby|perl|node|sh|bash)\b",
    ]).expect("heredoc patterns should compile")
});
```

### Quick Reject Logic

```rust
fn might_contain_heredoc(cmd: &str) -> bool {
    HEREDOC_PATTERNS.is_match(cmd)
}
```

Cost: **~1-10 microseconds** for typical commands.

## Tier 2: AST-Aware Analysis

Only invoked when Tier 1 detects a potential heredoc.

### Why ast-grep?

- **Structural matching**: Understands code structure, not just text
- **Language-aware**: Different patterns for Python vs Bash vs JS
- **Pattern syntax**: Intuitive `os.system($CMD)` instead of regex
- **Battle-tested**: Used in production for large-scale code search

### Example Flow

```rust
fn check_heredoc(cmd: &str) -> Option<CheckResult> {
    // Tier 1: Quick regex check
    if !might_contain_heredoc(cmd) {
        return None;  // Fast path: no heredoc
    }

    // Tier 2: Extract and analyze heredoc content
    let heredoc_info = extract_heredoc(cmd)?;
    let language = detect_language(&heredoc_info);

    // Parse with ast-grep
    let matches = match_destructive_patterns(&heredoc_info.content, language);

    if !matches.is_empty() {
        return Some(CheckResult::blocked(
            &format!("Heredoc contains dangerous pattern: {}", matches[0].reason),
            "heredoc"
        ));
    }

    None  // Heredoc is safe
}
```

## Performance Budget

| Operation | Budget | Typical |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Tier 1 regex check | <100μs | 1-10μs |
| Heredoc extraction | <1ms | 100-500μs |
| Language detection | <100μs | 10-50μs |
| AST parsing | <5ms | 500μs-2ms |
| Pattern matching | <1ms | 100-500μs |
| **Total (heredoc path)** | <10ms | 1-5ms |

## Implementation Priority

1. **First**: Implement Tier 1 with `regex` crate
2. **Second**: Add heredoc extraction logic
3. **Third**: Integrate ast-grep for Tier 2
4. **Fourth**: Optimize based on benchmarks

## Dependencies

```toml
# Tier 1: Fast regex
regex = "1.10"  # Already highly optimized

# Tier 2: AST analysis
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = ["bash", "python", "javascript"] }
```

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","title":"Epic: Test infrastructure + performance guardrails","description":"# Epic: Test infrastructure + performance guardrails

## Why this epic exists

`dcg` runs on every Bash command. That makes it more like a kernel hook than a typical CLI:

- it must never hang
- it must never panic
- it must have bounded worst-case latency

As we add complex logic (execution-context parsing, heredoc extraction, AST matching, repo scanning), the only sustainable way to maintain trust is to encode invariants and budgets in automated guardrails.

## Goals

1. Property-based tests for core invariants (determinism, idempotence, no panics).
2. Fuzzing harness for high-risk parsers/tokenizers/extractors.
3. Performance benchmarks with budgets (fast path and slow path) and CI enforcement.
4. Regression corpora for:
   - true positives
   - false positives
   - bypass attempts
   - edge cases

## Non-goals

- Chasing high coverage numbers at the expense of signal.
- Building a heavy telemetry system (optional later).

## Success Criteria

- CI can run a "core suite" quickly (unit + integration + key E2E) and a "deep suite" on schedule (fuzz/bench/proptest).
- Performance regressions are caught before release (budgets enforced, not just tracked).
- Any crash found by fuzzing is captured as a minimal repro and promoted into a regression test.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","title":"Property-based tests for evaluator invariants (proptest)","description":"## Objective

Add property-based tests that encode the invariants we never want to regress.

## Target invariants

1. Normalization idempotence:
   - `normalize(normalize(cmd)) == normalize(cmd)`
2. Determinism:
   - evaluating the same input twice yields the same decision and match identity
3. No panics:
   - evaluation never panics for arbitrary UTF-8 input
4. Bounded behavior:
   - overly large inputs are handled without pathological allocations

## Scope

- Focus on the shared evaluator entrypoint.
- Keep strategies controlled to avoid CI flakiness.
- Mark the slowest tests `#[ignore]` and run them in a deeper CI job.

## Acceptance criteria

- Proptest suite exists and runs locally.
- CI can run a small proptest subset reliably.
- Any discovered bug results in a reduced regression test.

Fixed Cow borrow issue by using into_owned(). All 103 lib + 92 main + 140 E2E tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:09.5251165-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:43.044726491-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:48.30731798-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -261,7 +261,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","title":"CLI: dcg allow-once <code>","description":"Provide a one-command UX to grant a temporary exception using the short code.","notes":"Scope:
- Command: `dcg allow-once <code>` (with --yes for non-interactive).
- Resolve code to pending record; if multiple, require disambiguation (list numbered options, support --pick). Default display uses redacted command; `--show-raw` required to print full command.
- Confirm by echoing exact command, cwd, and expiry timestamp.
- Support `--dry-run` and `--json` output for automation.
- Support `--single-use` to consume the exception after first allow (default is reusable until expiry).
- If the matching block is from explicit user-config blocklist, require `--force` plus extra confirmation.
- Write an ephemeral allowlist entry with expires_at = now+24h (and uses_remaining when single-use).

Acceptance criteria:
- User can copy the top-line code and allow the exact same command.
- Collisions are handled without ambiguity.
- Allowlist entry expires automatically and consumption is enforced when single-use.

Verification:
- Covered by git_safety_guard-oien.1.6 (unit) and git_safety_guard-oien.1.7 (e2e) using the shared harness/log schema.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.667348316-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:15:16.73266356-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:15:16.73266356-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented allow-once CLI flow + allow-once precedence/scope logic","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.668595956-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:11.474798319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","title":"Ephemeral allowlist layer + cwd scoping","description":"Add a temporary allowlist layer and enforce cwd/project scoping for allow-once entries.","notes":"Scope:
- Introduce an ephemeral allowlist layer with highest precedence among pack rules.
- Precedence: explicit user-config blocklist > allow-once > pack evaluation. Allow-once overrides pack-based denies only; use `--force` to override explicit blocklist.
- Allowlist entry includes scope conditions like `cwd` or `project_root`.
- Evaluation enforces scope before matching exact command.
- Expired entries are ignored and pruned; consumed entries are removed for single-use exceptions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Allow-once entry only matches in intended cwd (or project).
- Behavior is deterministic, audited in logs, and respects the chosen precedence policy.

Verification:
- Covered by git_safety_guard-oien.1.6 (unit) and git_safety_guard-oien.1.7 (e2e) using the shared harness/log schema.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.699271226-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:15:16.752667597-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:15:16.752667597-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented allow-once CLI flow + allow-once precedence/scope logic","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.700499339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:16.517427941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","title":"Hook output: top-line code + UX wording","description":"Place the allow-once code at the very top of the block output to avoid truncation.","notes":"Scope:
- Modify block output to prepend a single-line header: `ALLOW-24H CODE: abcd | run: dcg allow-once abcd`.
- Keep existing denial message intact below.
- Ensure no ANSI formatting breaks copy-paste.
- For non-TTY or JSON output, include code and full hash as structured fields (not buried in long strings).

Acceptance criteria:
- The code is always the first line of denial output.
- JSON output includes both code and full hash.
- No regression in existing denial formatting.

Verification:
- Add targeted unit tests for JSON fields and output ordering; run e2e harness to verify top-line code presence in logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.733222487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:54:14.060885546-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:54:14.060885546-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented allow-once header line + JSON fields + tests; fmt/check blocked by pre-existing heredoc.rs parse errors","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.734647742-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:21.562962946-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","title":"Unit tests: allow-once correctness","description":"Comprehensive unit tests for hashing, storage, scoping, and expiry.","notes":"Coverage checklist:
- Hash derivation uses exact input tuple (datetime|cwd|command) and optional HMAC secret if enabled.
- 4-char suffix selection is correct and lowercase.
- Collisions: multiple pending entries for same suffix resolve via disambiguation.
- Expiry: entries expire at 24h and are pruned.
- Scope enforcement: cwd mismatch does not allow.
- Redaction: default displays are redacted; `--show-raw` required to reveal full command.
- Default semantics: reusable-until-expiry is allowed on repeated runs.
- Single-use: `--single-use` consumes the entry; subsequent runs block.
- Precedence: explicit user-config blocklist is NOT overridden by allow-once unless `--force` is used; tests cover both paths.
- JSON output includes expected fields.
- Management commands (list/clear/revoke) behave deterministically.

Requirement: tests must use deterministic time (time mocking or injected clock) and reuse common test helpers where possible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.797304112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:50:43.488803648-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:50:43.488803648-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (hashing, scoping, expiry, disambiguation, precedence tests)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.798478354-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:31.653296368-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:36.699380107-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:41.742952515-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:46.787142707-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:41:20.48293591-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","title":"E2E tests: allow-once flow","description":"End-to-end tests that exercise the full block → code → allow-once → allow path.","notes":"Scenarios:
- Blocked command emits top-line code.
- `dcg allow-once CODE` allows the exact same command within 24h (repeatable until expiry).
- Same command outside cwd is still blocked.
- After expiry, command is blocked again.
- `--single-use` consumes the exception after first allow.
- Collision handling with two pending entries.
- Explicit user-config blocklist is not overridden; `--force` path is required and is covered in tests.
- `dcg allow-once list` shows pending + active entries (redacted by default).
- `dcg allow-once revoke` removes an active exception.

Logging:
- E2E logs must capture the top-line code, allowlist entry, management actions, and final decision.
- Prefer running via shared e2e harness to keep logs diffable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.83328164-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:48:54.675721361-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:48:54.675721361-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 10 E2E tests for allow-once flow: block→code→allow-once→allow path, cwd scoping, single-use consumption, force flag for config block, revoke, dry-run, JSON output, collision handling, and list command verification","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.834563285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:51.831183807-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:56.873707039-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:01.920769892-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:06.965363263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:41:25.530168568-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.8","title":"Documentation: allow-once usage + safety caveats","description":"Document the allow-once flow and the safety model for users.","notes":"Include:
- Example block output with code at top.
- The exact CLI command to allow.
- Expiry behavior and scope limitations.
- Default semantics: reusable until expiry; `--single-use` for one-shot.
- Redaction defaults and how to use --show-raw safely.
- Precedence: allow-once does not override explicit user-config blocklist unless --force.
- How to remove or list temporary exceptions.

Verification:
- Add a doc consistency check (unit test or CI step) that validates allow-once examples against actual CLI output; record outputs in logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.86544328-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:02:52.508896555-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.866749661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
